arts.ttl: Unai Emery drops Marco Asensio transfer hint as PSG reunion due
arts.sum: Unai Emery admitted Marco Asensio is “very important” to the structure of his team when asked if he wants to sign the playmaker permanently this summer. Aston Villa boss Unai Emery was asked if he wants to sign Marco Asensio permanently in the summer transfer window

arts.ttl: Thursday’s briefing: Arsenal and Villa advance as focus turns to Europa League
arts.sum: Manchester United, Tottenham and Rangers all play on Thursday night.

arts.ttl: Champions League quarterfinal predictions, from Real Madrid-Arsenal to Villa-PSG
arts.athr: Gab Marcotti and Mark Ogden and Julien Laurens and Rob Dawson and Beth Lindop
arts.sum: So, the round of 16 is over and after some amazing Champions League action, what will happen in the next round? ESPN FC's experts give their knee-jerk picks for the quarterfinals.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa earn shot at greatness after setting up historic PSG showdown | Jonathan Wilson
arts.sum: In reaching the Champions League quarter-finals, Unai Emery’s side have taken a step closer to being immortalised Glory comes in many forms. Perhaps the best Aston Villa could hope for on Wednesday was a game of little drama. They had in effect won the tie in Belgium last week; the last thing they wanted was to have to win it again. And yet, straightforward as it was, this was glorious, a night that in its outcome, if not the precise details, was epochal, marking Villa’s return to the European elite. Perhaps that will be a long‑term state, perhaps fleeting; either way, it is significant. There is a tide in the affairs of clubs which, taken at the flood, leads on to fortune. For Villa this was one of the nights fans yearn for, a night of destiny, a night to be spoken of for a long time to come. Even five years ago it would have seemed absurd that all that they had to do to reach the Champions League quarter-finals was avoid a two-goal defeat against the Belgian champions. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: 'We want more': Villa dreaming of toppling PSG
arts.sum: Aston Villa boss Unai Emery is thrilled to have steered his side into a Champions League quarterfinal, but now wants even more as they face a daunting clash with Paris Saint-Germain.

arts.ttl: Unai Emery proud as Aston Villa compete against the ‘best teams in the world’
arts.sum: Villa completed a 6-1 aggregate win over Club Brugge in the last-16.

arts.ttl: Why 'world class' Asensio can face employers PSG for Villa
arts.sum: Marco Asensio's Paris St-Germain career has been forgettable - but he now has the chance in the Champions League to make them remember his name.

arts.ttl: Asensio fires Villa ahead against Club Brugge
arts.sum: Marco Asensio grabs his sixth goal in eight appearances for Aston Villa with a sharp finish to put the 1982 European Cup winners 1-0 up against Club Brugge in the second leg of their Champions League last-16 tie.

arts.ttl: How 'revelation' Asensio changed the game for Aston Villa
arts.sum: Match of the Day pundits Shay Given and Julien Laurens analyse how Marco Asensio's half-time introduction helped change the game for Aston Villa in their 3-0 victory against Club Brugge in the Champions League last 16.
